reasoning: The 7-year Treasury yield recently closed at 3.91% (Dec 19, 2025), following a modest upward trend over the past month. The FRED statistical linear model gives a ~51% chance of being higher within a week, but macroeconomic factors skew slightly upward: the Dec 2025 FOMC meeting revealed the Fed median projection is for only one rate cut in 2026, while the market has been pricing in more dovish expectations (two cuts). As the market adjusts to this "higher for longer" consensus and rebuilds risk premia heading into 2026, yields are gently pushed upward. Therefore, my near-term forecast holds a slight positive bias (56%). Following instructions, uncertainty increases with horizon, washing out the directional bias, thereby bringing the probabilities closer to 0.5 for the 1-month and 3-month dates.
